description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
An armored external linear frame, integrated directly with the cyberware of the wearer for high performance.
Power fists (very heavy melee weapons) installed into the Brute's linear frame.
Increases your BODY stat to 12 (adjusting your HP and Wound Threshold and Death Save)
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A foldable bulletproof shield that can be drawn or stowed easily. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated.
Requires an arm and has HP that is reduced by damage.
Allows the brute to bash with a deployed popout shield, when not using it as cover.

Injecting a target with Speedheal immediately causes them to heal HP equal to their BODY plus their WILL. This effect does not work on Mortally Wounded targets, and can only be used on a target once per day.
Requires an arm and has HP that is reduced by damage.
Injecting a target with Stim causes them to ignore all penalties from being Seriously Wounded for one hour. It can only be used on a target once per day.
When the brute grapples an enemy, they automatically take 2d6 damage directly to their HP at the start of the brute's turn.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 5x/Special Boss Threat - An isolated, specialized, high-level agent that will likely pose a significant threat even without any backup.

Corp-Wide Ability - Advanced Tactics: Once per round after a Militech goon has taken an action, select another goon of the same type; they may immediately take the same action, if eligible to do so.

Ability - I’m The Juggernaut, Bitch: As an action, the brute expends their full movement in a linear direction, attacking anything in or adjacent to their path with 1 strike from a (4d6) heavy melee profile (the extra D6 is due to sheer mass and power) and knocking aside or plowing through anything which weighs 200 pounds or less (all actors are moved aside if hit, actors must make a DEX save; on failure, they are knocked prone). Damage dealt to objects (vehicles, walls, etc.) is doubled. When rolling endurance for suppressive effects, roll Physical instead of Lowdown. Brutes cannot be set on fire or poisoned.

Extra Gear - Choose one of the following:

  • Additional Shielding: Both of the brute’s arms are equipped with a popup shield.

    • Activate both simultaneously. If activated, the brute gains a medium melee weapon in both hands, and loses access to their power fists until the shield is put away or destroyed. For obvious reasons, the shield can’t be used in melee when the Brute is using it for cover.

  • Neuralstim: The Brute gains a speedheal and stim, which both automatically apply to their HP when they become seriously wounded.

  • Electrified Grips: When the brute grapples an enemy, they automatically take 1d6 damage directly to their HP at the start of the brute’s turn. This damage does not interact with armor, and if an enemy is incapacitated by it, is considered non-lethal.

If you see a brute, my advice is to find the nearest tank and put it between them, and you. That’s about the only thing that will stop one.
